decks.notes: The main concept of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive Boros creatures and burn spells. Utilizing cards like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury, along with Ajani, Nacatl Pariah for additional support, the deck aims to quickly lower the opponent's life total. Goblin Bombardment and Galvanic Discharge provide removal options, while cards like Arena of Glory and Voice of Victory help to boost your creatures and give them an advantage in combat. Overall, the strategy is to maintain a fast-paced, aggressive playstyle to defeat the opponent swiftly.

The main concept of this Dimir Control deck is to disrupt the opponent's strategy using a variety of counterspells, removal spells, and card draw effects. By controlling the pace of the game and efficiently dealing with threats, the deck aims to outvalue the opponent and win through incremental advantage. With a focus on card advantage and disruption, the deck utilizes a combination of powerful creatures and instant speed spells to maintain control of the battlefield and ultimately secure victory.

The deck focuses on utilizing aggressive creatures and burn spells to quickly deplete the opponent's life total. With cards like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls, the deck aims to apply early pressure and maintain a constant stream of threats. Cards like Goblin Bombardment and Galvanic Discharge provide additional reach to finish off the opponent. The inclusion of Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Voice of Victory allows for buffing up creatures and providing protection. The deck also includes disruption elements like Static Prison and Blood Moon to disrupt the opponent's game plan.

This deck aims to control the game by utilizing the UrzaTron lands to quickly ramp up mana and cast powerful Eldrazi creatures. It also includes disruptive cards like Thought-Knot Seer and Glaring Fleshraker to disrupt opponent's plans. The deck also features powerful planeswalkers like Ugin, the Spirit Dragon and Karn, the Great Creator to further control the battlefield. The goal is to outvalue the opponent with powerful threats and disruption, ultimately dominating the game through a combination of control elements and massive threats.

The deck focuses on playing an aggressive strategy utilizing artifact creatures and various artifacts to quickly overwhelm the opponent. Emry, Lurker of the Loch and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student provide card advantage and recursion, while cards like Metallic Rebuke and Unholy Heat offer disruption against opposing decks. With a mix of cheap artifacts and mana producers like Mox Opal, the deck aims to quickly establish a wide board presence and win the game before the opponent can stabilize. The combination of affinity synergies and disruptive elements makes this deck a potent force in Modern 1 vs 1 format.

The main concept of this Boros Aggro deck is to have a fast and aggressive strategy, using powerful creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls to quickly deal damage to the opponent. The deck also includes cards like Goblin Bombardment and Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury to control the board and maintain pressure. Additionally, cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Thraben Charm provide additional support and versatility in combat. Overall, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with an aggressive approach while also having some control elements to ensure victory.

The deck focuses on using cards like Galvanic Discharge and Indomitable Creativity to quickly ramp up mana and bring out powerful creatures like Archon of Cruelty. Leyline Binding and Fable of the Mirror-Breaker help control the opponent's board while Pawpatch Formation provides additional protection. Teferi, Time Raveler and Wrenn and Six offer additional utility and card advantage. The lands are carefully selected to ensure proper mana fixing and consistency. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of quick combo plays and versatile removal options.

The deck focuses on quickly generating mana with rituals and mana-producing artifacts to cast spells like Ral, Monsoon Mage, and Glimpse the Impossible. It also utilizes card draw and filtering effects to find combo pieces and eventually win the game using spells like Grapeshot. The deck aims to chain together spells efficiently to create a storm of powerful magic, overwhelming the opponent before they have a chance to respond. It also includes some land manipulation and card recursion to ensure consistency and resilience in executing its game plan.

The main focus of this Boros Aggro deck is to quickly overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and burn spells. Utilizing cards like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls to apply early pressure, while also having access to removal spells like Galvanic Discharge and Thraben Charm. The deck also includes cards like Goblin Bombardment and Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury to deal additional damage and close out games. With a mix of creatures, burn spells, and disruptive elements, this deck aims to outpace and outmaneuver the opponent to secure victories in a Modern 1 vs 1 format.

This deck relies on utilizing Song of Creation to draw cards and generate mana, allowing for explosive plays and potentially infinite combos with Mox Opal and Mox Amber. Emry, Lurker of the Loch helps recur key artifacts while Urza's Saga generates additional artifacts to trigger Song of Creation. With a mix of control elements like Unholy Heat and disruption with Endurance, the deck aims to overwhelm opponents quickly with powerful synergies and card advantage. Malevolent Rumble and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student provide additional value and utility, rounding out the strategy for a versatile and potent combo deck.

This deck focuses on aggressive creatures paired with blink effects to repeatedly trigger powerful enter-the-battlefield abilities. By using cards like Ephemerate and Flickerwisp, you can protect your creatures, disrupt your opponent's strategy, and generate value with cards like Overlord of the Balemurk and Quantum Riddler. The combination of aggression and disruption allows you to quickly build a board presence while denying your opponent the ability to stabilize. With a mix of efficient removal spells and powerful blink effects, this deck aims to overwhelm your opponent with value and tempo.

Utilizing a combination of aggressive creatures and blink effects, this deck aims to overwhelm opponents with a fast and disruptive gameplay. By blinking creatures like Ragavan and Phelia, the deck can generate value and tempo advantage, while also having access to removal and control elements with cards like Solitude and Prismatic Ending. The deck also features powerful finishers such as Phlage and Arena of Glory to seal the game quickly. With a mix of aggression and disruption, this deck aims to outpace opponents and secure a swift victory.

The deck focuses on quickly filling the graveyard with cycling creatures and then casting Living End to bring them all back onto the battlefield at once, overwhelming the opponent with a powerful board presence. It utilizes various cards with cycling abilities to maneuver through the deck efficiently while also providing disruption with counterspells and removal spells. The goal is to constantly cycle through the deck, setting up for a big Living End play that can swing the game in your favor. The deck's strategy revolves around synergy between cycling creatures and the combo potential of Living End.

The main concept of this Boros Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of aggressive creatures and powerful instant/sorcery spells. Utilizing cards like Ajani, Guide of Souls, and Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer, the deck aims to quickly build a board presence and deal damage efficiently. Alongside disruptive spells like Goblin Bombardment and Thraben Charm, the deck maintains control while pressuring the opponent. Arena of Glory provides additional strength to creatures, while cards like Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury and Voice of Victory offer late-game finishers. The deck is designed for fast-paced, aggressive gameplay in the Modern format.

The main focus of this Ruby Storm deck is to efficiently cast a large number of spells in a single turn to generate a massive amount of mana, allowing the player to cast powerful spells like Grapeshot to deal lethal damage to the opponent. The deck utilizes cards like Ruby Medallion, Pyretic Ritual, and Desperate Ritual to generate mana quickly, while also including cards like Ral, Monsoon Mage and Wrenn's Resolve to draw and filter through the deck. The goal is to create a fast and explosive combo turn to overwhelm the opponent and secure victory.

The deck focuses on aggressively attacking the opponent while also utilizing blinking effects to protect key creatures and disrupt the opponent's game plan. It aims to control the board with a mix of removal spells and powerful creatures like Phelia and Phlage, while also generating card advantage through Ephemerate and Consign to Memory. The deck also includes a flexible mana base to support its aggressive game plan and ensure it can cast its powerful spells.
